parameters.content:
## 📰 Auto-Post Blog Content to LinkedIn & Twitter/X

**What this workflow does:**
This template monitors your blog's RSS feed for new posts, extracts the title, summary, and cover image, uploads the image to a public URL via the UploadToURL node, then generates platform-optimized captions using AI and auto-posts to both LinkedIn and Twitter/X — all hands-free.

**Trigger:** RSS Feed polling (every 15 minutes)

**Key nodes used:**
- 🔁 RSS Feed Trigger
- 🧹 Data Extraction & Cleanup (Code Node)
- ☁️ UploadToURL (mandatory image hosting)
- 🤖 AI Caption Generator (OpenAI)
- 🔗 LinkedIn Post
- 🐦 Twitter/X Post
- ✅ Success Logger

**Setup Requirements:**
1. Add your RSS Feed URL in the RSS Trigger node
2. Configure OpenAI API credentials
3. Add LinkedIn OAuth2 credentials
4. Add Twitter/X OAuth1 credentials
5. UploadToURL node requires no extra auth — paste your upload endpoint

**Note:** Deduplication logic is built-in via item uniqueness check to avoid re-posting old articles.

### 🔁 Step 1 — RSS Trigger & Filter
**RSS Feed Trigger:** Polls your blog RSS every 15 min for new items.
**IF Node:** Checks that both `title` and `enclosure` (cover image URL) are present. If no image is found, the workflow exits gracefully — no broken posts.

### ☁️ Step 2 — Fetch & Upload Image
**HTTP Request (Fetch Image):** Downloads the raw binary of the blog cover image from the enclosure URL.
**UploadToURL Node:** Takes the binary image data and uploads it to your configured public storage endpoint. Returns a hosted public URL used in social posts.

### 🤖 Step 3 — AI Caption + Posting
**OpenAI Node:** Generates two platform-specific captions — one for LinkedIn (professional tone, 150-200 words, with hashtags) and one for Twitter/X (punchy, under 260 chars with 2-3 hashtags).
**LinkedIn + Twitter/X Nodes:** Post the AI-generated captions with the hosted image URL. Both run in parallel after the AI step.

nodes.name: Code — Merge Hosted URL with Blog Data

main.node: Code — Merge Hosted URL with Blog Data
